title: Full Adder Logic Circuit
examTip: Sum = A ⊕ B ⊕ Cin. Cout = AB + Cin(A ⊕ B). A Full Adder can be constructed using two Half Adders and an OR gate. Memorize this cascade for exam questions.
theory:
A Full Adder is a combinational digital logic circuit that performs the addition of three input bits: A, B, and a Carry-in (Cin) from a previous addition stage. It produces two outputs: Sum (S) and Carry-out (Cout).

Logic Implementation:
A standard Full Adder is constructed using two Half Adders and an OR gate:
1. First Half Adder: Adds inputs A and B, producing a temporary sum (S1 = A ⊕ B) and a temporary carry (C1 = A · B).
2. Second Half Adder: Adds S1 and Cin, producing the final Sum (S = S1 ⊕ Cin = A ⊕ B ⊕ Cin) and a secondary carry (C2 = S1 · Cin).
3. OR Gate: Combines the carry outputs from both Half Adders to produce the final Carry-out (Cout = C1 + C2 = AB + Cin(A ⊕ B)).

This cascading structure allows Full Adders to be connected in series to perform multi-bit binary addition (e.g., Ripple Carry Adders).
keyPoints: Adds three binary inputs: A, B, and Carry-in (Cin)
Produces two outputs: Sum (Sum) and Carry-out (Cout)
Sum logic equation: Sum = A ⊕ B ⊕ Cin
Carry-out logic equation: Cout = A·B + Cin·(A ⊕ B)
Can be built using two Half Adders and a single OR gate
useCases: Cascaded in Ripple Carry Adders to add multi-bit binary numbers
Utilized in Arithmetic Logic Units (ALUs) for integer arithmetic
Incorporated into multiplier circuits and DSP processors
